confirmed.correction: Two minor refinements: (1) The per-prayer checklist keys (slotKey(hijri.year, d.day, prayer)) are keyed by the Hijri day number of the tapped row, not today's computed date, so saved data is not corrupted by the offset; the harm is that window gating, the 'Active now — Day X' banner, TODAY highlight, and the afterTashreeq cutoff point at the wrong day, leading users to act on/check the wrong day. (2) For Indo-Pak users the default seeded offset is -1 (tabular runs ahead of local moonsighting), so the unoffset screens show the window one day EARLY (and end Takbir on local day 12/13 boundary early), not late as '+1' in the claim implies — direction depends on the user's offset sign. Additionally (separate latent issue, consistent with this repo's known tab keep-alive bug class): the useMemo with [] deps means `hijri` never recomputes while the screen stays mounted, so the date can also go stale across midnight.

confirmed.title: Sacred Journey hero shows '00 days 00 hrs 00 mins' plus a past date for the entire duration of multi-day events (all of Ramadan, all 10 days of Dhul Hijjah)
confirmed.description: getUpcomingEvents keeps a multi-day event in the list until its endDate (`if (e.effectiveEndDate) return e.effectiveEndDate >= today`), and it sorts first by start date, so during Ramadan 1448 (Feb 8 – Mar 9, 2027) the hero card is pinned to 'Ramadan' for 30 straight days. computeCountdown counts down to effectiveDate (the START), which is already past, so it returns isNow with days/hours/minutes all 0. The hero renders three '00' blocks plus the start date string ('Monday, February 8' shown on Feb 25) — it looks frozen/broken for a month, hides the Laylat al-Qadr countdowns behind it, and getQuoteForEvent(type, 0) pins the same quote the whole time. NextEventCard on Home shows 'Today' for the same month. Same applies to 'First 10 Days of Dhul Hijjah' (10 days of zeros).
confirmed.evidence:
islamicCalendar.ts:323 `if (e.effectiveEndDate) return e.effectiveEndDate >= today;`
SacredJourneyScreen.tsx:55 `if (liveDiff <= 0) return { days: 0, hours: 0, minutes: 0, isNow: true };`
HeroCard renders <CountdownBlock value={countdown.days} .../> with no isNow branch — String(0).padStart(2,'0') → '00' for all three blocks, and heroDate shows event.effectiveDate (the past start date).
confirmed.suggestedFix: When countdown.isNow and the event has an effectiveEndDate, render an 'ongoing — day N of M' state (or count down to the end/next sub-event) instead of the 00/00/00 blocks, and show 'started <date>' rather than the raw start date.
confirmed.correction: Two minor overstatements: (1) Laylat al-Qadr events are not hidden — they still appear with countdowns in the 'The Journey Ahead' timeline (upcomingRest, SacredJourneyScreen.tsx:290-301); they just never become the hero card. (2) NextEventCard's 'Today' is an intentional isNow branch — misleading for a month-long ongoing event, but not the frozen '00 00 00' rendering, which is hero-only. Also, the bug is future-dated: nothing is currently mid-event (today 2026-06-10); first manifestation is Ramadan starting Feb 8, 2027 (Dhul Hijjah 1447 already passed in May 2026, so it would have shown then too). Otherwise the claim is accurate, including high severity for ~40 days/year of broken-looking hero during peak feature relevance.

confirmed.title: Sacred Journey hero countdown overstates remaining time by ~1 day (calendar-days mixed with real hours) and disagrees with the Home card
confirmed.description: computeCountdown derives `days` from midnight-normalized dates (counts today as a full day) but `hours`/`minutes` from raw timestamps mod 24/60. Example: now = Jun 10 14:00, event Jun 16 00:00 → real remaining = 5d 10h, but display = '06 days 10 hrs' — the sum is wrong by exactly one day at almost every moment. It also produces a weird midnight rollover: 23:59 the day before shows '06d 00h 01m', then a minute later '05d 23h 59m' after briefly showing '05d 00h'. Meanwhile NextEventCard on Home computes pure real-time diff ('5d · 10h'), so the two countdowns for the same event differ by a day, and the share text uses the inflated numbers ('Eid — 6 days, 10 hours').
confirmed.evidence:
const days = Math.floor(diffMs / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24));            // midnight-normalized
const hours = Math.floor((target.getTime() - now.getTime()) / (1000 * 60 * 60)) % 24;  // raw diff
vs NextEventCard.tsx:23 days: Math.floor(diff / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24)) on raw diff
confirmed.suggestedFix: Pick one semantic: either pure duration (days/hours/minutes all from the raw diff, like NextEventCard) or pure calendar days with no hour/minute blocks. Don't mix.

confirmed.title: Hajj/Umrah checklist progress is never persisted — multi-day Hajj progress lost on back-navigation or app restart
confirmed.description: Both guide screens present a progress bar, 'Mark as Complete' buttons and a completion celebration, but completedIds is plain useState with no storage read/write. These are stack screens that unmount on back, so merely leaving the screen wipes all checkmarks. Hajj is a 6-day, 14-step ritual where a pilgrim would realistically check steps across days 8–13 Dhul Hijjah — every visit starts at 0/14. TakbirScreen (the sibling tracker) persists to AsyncStorage, so users will expect the same. (JanazaScreen has the same pattern but is plausibly single-sitting.)
confirmed.suggestedFix: Persist the completed set to AsyncStorage keyed per guide (and rehydrate on mount), with a small 'Reset' action; auto-expire after the season like TakbirScreen prunes old Hijri years.

confirmed.title: Qurbani people-counter desyncs when switching animals: '+' button decreases the value and share visualizer claims '10 of 7 shares'
confirmed.description: With a goat selected the cap is 10 (people can be 8–10). Switching to cow/buffalo/camel does NOT clamp people (only `if (a.shares === 1) setPeople(1)` exists for the reverse direction). With people=10 and cow selected: the share row renders only 7 PersonIcons (all filled) with the label '10 of 7 shares claimed', and pressing '+' executes Math.min(7, 11) — the counter visibly JUMPS DOWN from 10 to 7. The dead 'Re-clamp' comment block at lines 201–205 shows the case was noticed but the body is empty.
confirmed.suggestedFix: On animal switch, clamp: setPeople((n) => Math.min(n, a.shares === 1 ? 10 : 7)); or render the visualizer with Math.min(people, 7) and clamp in an effect keyed on selectedKey.
confirmed.correction: Core finding is accurate. Two minor nuances: (1) the dead 'Re-clamp' comment block at lines 201-205 concerns the reverse (7-share → goat) direction, where keeping people>1 is intentionally valid ("allow many goats"), so it doesn't directly evidence that the cow-direction case was noticed; (2) the numeric result line stays correct (2 cows for 10 people), so the bug is confined to the share visualizer label and the counter jumping 10→7 on '+' — severity medium is fair but at the low end.

confirmed.title: Qurbani countdown banner renders blank after May 2027 — no Eid al-Adha entries beyond 2027
confirmed.description: UPCOMING_EID_DATES contains only two Adha dates (May 27 2026, May 17 2027) plus Fitr 2028. After the 1448 Adha passes, nextEidAdha() returns null, daysUntil is null, labels.windowSoon becomes '' — and outside the slaughter window the banner renders an hourglass icon next to empty text. The `windowClosed` label is defined but unreachable (the banner only ever picks windowOpen or windowSoon).
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add Eid al-Adha 2028 (May 5, 2028 — already present in islamicCalendar.ts as adha-1449) to UPCOMING_EID_DATES, or derive the date from ISLAMIC_EVENTS instead of a second hand-maintained table; hide the banner (or use windowClosed) when daysUntil is null.

confirmed.title: Takbir/Qurbani Hijri day frozen at mount — TODAY highlight and window banners go stale across midnight
confirmed.description: `gregorianToHijri(new Date())` is memoized with an empty dependency array and there is no tick or focus refresh. The Takbir tracker is exactly the screen a user keeps open across days (mark Isha at night, Fajr next morning): if the screen instance survives overnight (app backgrounded, not killed), the 'TODAY' row highlight, the 'Active now — Day X' banner, and afterTashreeq state all still show yesterday, and tapped chips are keyed to the right day only because the user must scroll to the correct row manually. QurbaniScreen.tsx:82 has the identical frozen pattern for its window banners.
confirmed.evidence:
const hijri = useMemo(() => gregorianToHijri(new Date()), []);
// no setInterval / useFocusEffect updating any date state in TakbirScreen
confirmed.suggestedFix: Recompute hijri from a `now` state refreshed by useFocusEffect and/or an AppState 'active' listener (a minute interval like SacredJourneyScreen also works).
confirmed.correction: Minor nuance only: these are stack screens, not tab screens, so the staleness requires the screen to remain mounted (open on top or app backgrounded with it on the stack) across midnight; any re-navigation remounts and self-heals. The claim already frames it this way, so no substantive correction needed.

confirmed.title: Arabic-language users get English/Urdu-only text in Sacred Journey share and quotes
confirmed.description: The screen fully supports 'ar' for UI labels, but onShareNext builds the countdown string and quote with only isUrdu/English branches, so an Arabic-locale user shares an English message ('3 days, 4 hours' + English quote). Same for the HeroCard quote body (line 399: `isUrdu ? quote.ur : quote.en`) — SacredQuote has no `ar` translation field at all (only optional `arabic` source text), so the rendered quote body under an Arabic UI is English.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add isArabic branches to countdownStr/share text, and either add `ar` strings to SacredQuote or fall back to the quote's `arabic` source text for Arabic users.
confirmed.correction: Claim is accurate with one small omission: in the HeroCard, quote.arabic (original Arabic source text) is rendered above the translation body when present (lines 429-430), so Arabic UI users do see Arabic scripture in the card; only the translation line and the entire share message lack Arabic.

confirmed.title: Region change in Sacred Journey can silently leave notifications scheduled for the old region's dates
confirmed.description: pickRegion is an async function invoked fire-and-forget from onPress (line 343: `onPress={() => pickRegion(r.id)}`) with no try/catch and no .catch — unlike toggleMute/toggleMaster which call persistAndReschedule(...).catch(() => {}). requestNotificationPermissions, setupNotificationChannel and scheduleSacredCountdownNotifications all await native APIs that can reject (getPermissionsAsync, setNotificationChannelAsync, getAllScheduledNotificationsAsync are uncaught inside notifications.ts). If any rejects, the rejection is unhandled and — since setRegion/setCalendarRegion already succeeded — the UI shows the new region while the sacred-event notifications remain scheduled against the OLD region's dates (a 1-day-off Eid notification for a South Asia ↔ Saudi switch), with no error surfaced.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Wrap the reschedule block in try/catch (mirroring persistAndReschedule's .catch) and surface a non-blocking retry toast on failure, or route pickRegion through persistAndReschedule.
confirmed.correction: The issue is real but its persistence is overstated as "silently leave notifications scheduled for the old region's dates" without qualification. pickRegion awaits storage.setCalendarRegion(r) BEFORE the scheduling step, so the new region is persisted even on scheduling failure, and PrayerTrackerScreen.tsx lines 153-207 re-runs scheduleSacredCountdownNotifications with the stored (new) region on every fresh mount of that screen and on each new day / settings / location change (lastScheduledKey is a ref that resets on remount). The stale old-region schedule therefore self-heals on the next app launch / next day rather than persisting indefinitely; a wrong-date notification only fires if a milestone lands in that window AND the rare native-API rejection occurred. Also, a mid-loop failure could leave a partial mix (old ones already cancelled, new ones partially scheduled), not strictly "old region's dates".

confirmed.title: Urdu UI shows untranslated English for women's notes (Hajj/Umrah) and Shia notes (Janaza); Umrah phase label dropped entirely in Urdu
confirmed.description: UmrahStep.womenNote, HajjStep.womenNote and JanazaStep.shiaNote exist only in English (no *Ur variants in the type or data), yet the screens render them under Urdu headers in Urdu mode — e.g. 'خواتین کے لیے: ' followed by an English paragraph, and the Janaza 'جعفری فقہ' box with an English body. Additionally UmrahGuideScreen deliberately renders an empty string instead of the phase label in Urdu (`{isUrdu ? '' : step.phase}`), losing the Before Travel/Ihram/Tawaf/Sa'i grouping that HajjGuideScreen does provide via dayUr. The Janaza Urdu disclaimer explicitly promises 'شیعہ (جعفری) فقہ کے فرق واضح نشان کے ساتھ درج ہیں' but delivers them in English.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add womenNoteUr / shiaNoteUr / phaseUr fields to the constants and fall back to English only when missing (the screens already use that pattern for tipsUr).

confirmed.title: Copy buttons in Janaza/Hajj/Umrah give no success feedback and swallow failures
confirmed.description: The Copy actions call Clipboard.setStringAsync fire-and-forget: no 'Copied' confirmation and an unhandled promise if the native call rejects. QurbaniScreen's onCopyNiyyah shows an Alert with labels.copied on success, so the app is inconsistent — at a funeral or in ihram a user taps Copy and gets zero indication anything happened. Same pattern in UmrahGuideScreen.tsx:124 and HajjGuideScreen.tsx:126.
confirmed.evidence:
onPress={() => Clipboard.setStringAsync(step.arabic ?? '')}
// vs QurbaniScreen.tsx:187-192: await Clipboard.setStringAsync(text); Alert.alert('', labels.copied);
confirmed.suggestedFix: Reuse the Qurbani pattern (await + lightweight confirmation/toast, catch ignored) in the three guide screens' copy handlers.
confirmed.correction: Claim is accurate as stated. Two minor refinements: the same fire-and-forget pattern also exists in src/screens/DuaScreen.tsx:223 (not cited in the claim), and the "unhandled promise" risk is theoretical — expo-clipboard setStringAsync virtually never rejects for plain strings and an unhandled rejection in RN is a warning, not a crash. The substantive issue is the missing 'Copied' feedback and inconsistency with QurbaniScreen.

confirmed.title: SURAH_JUZ table assigns wrong juz to surahs 8, 67-70, and 78-80
confirmed.description: The hardcoded starting-juz table is factually wrong for 8 surahs. Surah 8 (Al-Anfal) starts in juz 9 but is listed as 10. Surahs 67-70 (Al-Mulk, Al-Qalam, Al-Haqqah, Al-Ma'arij) start juz 29 but are listed as 28. Surahs 78-80 (An-Naba, An-Nazi'at, Abasa) start juz 30 ('Amma juz, the most commonly memorized) but are listed as 29. So the 'Juz 30' filter omits An-Naba/An-Nazi'at/Abasa, 'Juz 29' shows the wrong set, and the per-juz '✓ memorized' badge fires for the wrong surah groups. Hifz students know these boundaries cold — this is immediately visible wrong data. (Surah 51 is also debatable: 51:1 falls in juz 26, listed as 27.)
confirmed.suggestedFix: Correct the entries: index 7 (surah 8) → 9; indices 66-69 (surahs 67-70) → 29; indices 77-79 (surahs 78-80) → 30. Add a unit test asserting known boundaries (67→29, 78→30, 114→30).
confirmed.correction: Claim is accurate as stated. Minor refinement: the surahs 67-70 and 78-80 entries are wrong under any convention (those surahs are entirely within juz 29 and 30 respectively), while surah 8 (mostly in juz 9, partially juz 10) and surah 51 (starts in juz 26, mostly in juz 27) are wrong specifically against the file's declared "starting juz" semantics. The bug affects filtering and the juz-memorized badge only; saved hifz progress data itself is keyed by surah and unaffected.

confirmed.title: Juz chips 2, 5, 9 always show '✓' (memorized) and filter to an empty list
confirmed.description: No surah in SURAH_JUZ starts in juz 2, 5, or 9 (juz 2 begins mid-Baqarah, etc.). For those chips the filter yields an empty array and Array.prototype.every on an empty array returns true, so the chip renders '✓ Juz 2' in the green 'done' style even for a brand-new user who has memorized nothing. Tapping the chip shows a blank list (the FlatList has no ListEmptyComponent), looking broken.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Guard with length: `const group = SURAH_LIST.filter(...); const juzMem = group.length > 0 && group.every(...)`. Either hide chips whose group is empty or map every juz to the surahs it contains (not just surahs that start in it).

confirmed.title: TodayCard 'Today's Act' freezes at first render — never updates across day boundaries
confirmed.description: The act is memoized with deps [sect, hijriOffset] only; `new Date()` is captured when the memo first computes. HomeScreen is a keep-alive tab that never unmounts (the component's own comment at line 45 says so), and the useFocusEffect only re-reads sect — setSect with an identical value bails out of re-render, and even on re-render the memo returns the cached act because its deps haven't changed. Result: a user whose app process survives midnight (very common on Android) sees Thursday's act on Friday (no Jumu'ah/Surah al-Kahf card), or yesterday's sacred-day card on Eid/Arafah/Ashura — the exact day-sensitive content this card exists for.
confirmed.evidence: const act: DailyAct = useMemo(() => getTodayAct(new Date(), sect, hijriOffset), [sect, hijriOffset]);
confirmed.suggestedFix: Track the current day key in state and refresh it inside the existing useFocusEffect (and/or an AppState 'active' listener): `const [dayKey, setDayKey] = useState(getLocalDateKey()); ... setDayKey(getLocalDateKey());` then include dayKey in the useMemo deps.
confirmed.correction: Essentially accurate. One small overstatement: the act doesn't freeze at the literal first render — it recomputes whenever sect hydrates (null→value just after mount) or hijriOffset changes; in practice it freezes at the last such change, typically moments after mount, which doesn't change the impact. The same frozen-date pattern also affects HomeScreen's Hijri header memo at HomeScreen.tsx:66 (deps [language, hijriOffset]), which the claim didn't mention.

confirmed.title: Hadith of the Day never rotates while the app stays alive
confirmed.description: getDailyHadith() is memoized with an empty dependency array. HadithOfDay is rendered by HomeScreen (a keep-alive tab that never unmounts), so the hadith picked on first mount is shown forever until the process is killed. Users who keep the app in background across days see the same 'Hadith of the Day' every day, defeating the feature. Same keep-alive stale-read class the project memory flags.
confirmed.evidence: const hadith = useMemo(() => getDailyHadith(), []);
confirmed.suggestedFix: Re-derive on focus/day change: lift a `dayKey` (getLocalDateKey()) refreshed via useFocusEffect or an AppState listener and use it as the useMemo dependency, mirroring the fix needed in TodayCard.

confirmed.title: Tasbih 'Today' count does not reset at midnight while the screen stays mounted
confirmed.description: The date check for todayCount runs only in the mount-time hydration effect. If the user has TasbihScreen open (or backgrounded — the screen stays mounted while the app is backgrounded) across midnight, todayCount keeps accumulating yesterday's beads. Worse, flushPending stamps the record with todayKey() evaluated at flush time, so yesterday's accumulated count is persisted under today's date: `{date: todayKey(), count: s.todayCount}`. Next morning the user sees e.g. 'Today: 500' before doing any dhikr, and the corrupt record survives restart because it now carries today's date.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Store the date the count belongs to in state (e.g. keep a `todayDate` alongside todayCount). In increment() and in the AppState 'active' handler, compare against todayKey() and reset todayCount to 0 when the day has rolled over; persist using the stored date, not todayKey() at flush time.
confirmed.correction: Minor framing fix: TasbihScreen is not a keep-alive tab — it is a pushed Stack.Screen (App.tsx lines 309-313) and unmounts on back-navigation. The exposure is the screen left open or the app backgrounded from Tasbih across midnight (screens stay mounted while the app is backgrounded). Also, the unmount-cleanup flush itself can stamp yesterday's count with today's date if the user navigates back after midnight. Core bug and medium severity are accurate.

confirmed.title: Hifz progress ring shows ~25% filled at 0% progress
confirmed.description: The ring is faked with four border quadrants. borderColor is set to accent unconditionally and only the top/right/bottom borders are conditionally dimmed, so borderLeftColor is always accent — a user with 0 surahs memorized sees a quarter-filled ring next to '0%'. The ring also only has 25% granularity (1 memorized surah = 1% shows the same arc as 24%).
confirmed.evidence:
<View style={[styles.progressRingFill, {
  borderColor: theme.colors.accent,
  borderTopColor: progressPct > 25 ? theme.colors.accent : theme.colors.backgroundSoft,
  borderRightColor: progressPct > 50 ? theme.colors.accent : theme.colors.backgroundSoft,
  borderBottomColor: progressPct > 75 ? theme.colors.accent : theme.colors.backgroundSoft,
}]} />
confirmed.suggestedFix: Gate the left quadrant too (`borderLeftColor: progressPct > 0 ? accent : backgroundSoft`), or reuse the existing SVG StreakRing component which renders true fractional progress.

confirmed.title: MoodScreen mood submission has no catch — storage failure is silent and rejects unhandled
confirmed.description: handleMoodSelect is try/finally with no catch. If storage.getPrayers/getMoods/setMoods throws (AsyncStorage I/O failure — matches the user complaint that 'random things fail'), the rejection propagates unhandled out of the onPress handler, the 'thinking' spinner disappears, messages stay cleared (line 43 wiped them), and the user is left staring at an empty chat with no error message and no saved mood entry.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add a catch that still shows the motivation message (it is computed locally before the write) and/or surfaces a brief 'could not save' notice; at minimum `catch (e) {}` to avoid the unhandled rejection while keeping the already-set messages.
confirmed.correction: Two details are overstated. (1) The user is not left with a literally empty chat: when thinking=false and messages=[], MoodScreen renders the dashed placeholder card with moodPlaceholder copy (lines 110-115) — still no error indication, but not blank. (2) The 'messages stay cleared' outcome only applies if the failure occurs at getPrayers (line 46). If getMoods/setMoods (lines 66-68) throws, setMessages([response]) at line 64 has already run, so the user sees the motivation response and the only effect is the mood entry silently failing to persist. The no-catch / silent-failure / unhandled-rejection core of the claim is accurate.

confirmed.title: StatsScreen data load has no error handling — failure leaves all stats at 0 silently
confirmed.description: The focus-driven async IIFE awaits four storage reads with no try/catch and no .catch on the IIFE. Any rejection is unhandled, and because state was initialized to zeros, the screen confidently displays '0 day streak / 0%' — indistinguishable from real data loss to the user, and partial state is possible (streak set, consistency stale) since reads are sequential.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Wrap the body in try/catch; on failure keep previous values (don't reset to 0) and optionally show a lightweight 'couldn't load stats' state.
confirmed.correction: Two minor inaccuracies: (1) the realistic failure surface is narrower than implied — corrupt stored JSON (the most common storage failure) is already swallowed by parseJson with a safe fallback in src/services/storage.ts, so only a native AsyncStorage.getItem rejection (rare disk/IO failure) triggers the unhandled rejection; (2) the partial-state example 'streak set, consistency stale' is wrong — streak and prayerConsistency are both derived from the first Promise.all batch and set before the next await; the actual stale pair on a later rejection would be goalsThisWeek and moodAvg (if getGoals or getMoods rejects after streak/consistency were already set).

confirmed.title: Goals-this-week uses UTC parsing of local date keys, contradicting the project's own local-date rule
confirmed.description: g.date is a local YYYY-MM-DD key (getLocalDateKey), but `new Date(g.date)` parses it as UTC midnight, then compares against a local `weekAgo` timestamp. In negative-UTC timezones (US users) a goal completed exactly 7 local days ago can be counted or dropped depending on the time of day; streak.ts's daysApart() contains an explicit comment warning against this exact pitfall, so this call site missed the fix.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Compare date keys lexically instead: compute `const weekAgoKey = getLocalDateKey(addLocalDays(new Date(), -7))` and filter with `g.date >= weekAgoKey`.
confirmed.correction: Minor detail: in negative-UTC zones the goal completed exactly 7 local days ago is always excluded (not time-of-day dependent), because its UTC-midnight parse lands |offset| hours before that day's local midnight while weekAgo carries the current time-of-day. The time-of-day-dependent flip in negative-UTC zones actually affects the 6-days-ago key (dropped once local time passes 24h minus |offset|, e.g. after 17:00 in UTC-7); in positive-UTC zones it's the 7-days-ago key that flips. The core finding — UTC parsing of a local date key makes the boundary-day count vary by timezone and time of day, inconsistent with the project's own daysApart() fix — stands.

confirmed.title: Mood average aggregates the last 7 entries, not the last 7 days
confirmed.description: MoodScreen unshifts a new entry on every tap with no per-day dedup (`moods.unshift({ date: today, mood, aiResponse: response })` in MoodScreen.tsx line 67), so a user who taps moods 7 times in one sitting makes 'Mood average' reflect a single minute rather than a week. The stat reads as a weekly average but is an entry-count average.
confirmed.evidence: const recentMoods = moods.slice(0, 7).filter((m) => m.mood);
confirmed.suggestedFix: Either dedupe per day in MoodScreen (replace today's entry instead of unshifting a new one) or aggregate by unique date here: group entries by m.date, take the latest per day for the last 7 calendar days.
confirmed.correction: Minor nuance only: the UI label (translations.ts:150) is just 'Mood Average', not 'Mood Average (7 days)', so the stat does not literally claim to be weekly — the weekly reading is implied by sitting beside the explicitly 7-day prayer-consistency card. The mechanical finding (entry-count average, not day-based, no dedup) is fully accurate.

confirmed.title: Tasbih taps in the first moments after open are silently erased by hydration
confirmed.description: The counter button is interactive immediately, but the async hydration effect later calls setCount(saved.count), setLifetime(Number(rawLife)), setTodayCount(t.count) unconditionally — overwriting any beads counted before AsyncStorage resolved. On a slow device or busy bridge (the comment at line 95 says the bridge does back up on Android), the first taps of a dhikr session vanish.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Use functional merges during hydration (e.g. `setCount((c) => c + (saved.count ?? 0))` is wrong for count but right for lifetime/today deltas) — simplest correct fix: disable the counter button until `hydrated` is true, or hydrate before first paint via a loading gate.
confirmed.correction: Minor scope correction: the loss only occurs for users with previously saved state. On a fresh install rawState is null, the `if (rawState)` guard skips setCount, and pre-hydration taps survive (count); lifetime/today similarly only overwritten if their keys exist. For returning users (the common case) the claim holds exactly as described.

confirmed.title: Hifz/Asma persistence errors are swallowed — progress can be silently lost
confirmed.description: cycleStatus updates UI state then fires AsyncStorage.setItem with `.catch(() => {})`. If the write fails the user sees the surah marked 'Memorized', but it reverts on next launch with no indication anything went wrong. Same pattern in AsmaulHusnaScreen.tsx line 88 (`AsyncStorage.setItem(KNOWN_KEY, ...).catch(() => {})`) and TasbihScreen flushPending line 115. Matches the dev's reported class of 'random things fail' with no visible error.
confirmed.evidence: AsyncStorage.setItem(HIFZ_KEY, JSON.stringify(next)).catch(() => {});
confirmed.suggestedFix: At minimum log the error; better, retry once and on persistent failure show a one-time toast ('Couldn't save progress') so users know to report it rather than experiencing mystery data loss.
confirmed.correction: Claim is accurate as stated. Minor nuance: because every write persists the full state snapshot, a single transient failure is self-corrected by the next successful write; data is only lost if the final write before app exit fails or failures persist (e.g. disk full). This supports, rather than contradicts, the 'low' severity rating.

confirmed.title: Juz chip row runs 30 × 114-item filter+every scans on every render
confirmed.description: Inside the render JSX, each of the 30 juz chips filters all 114 surahs and runs .every — ~3,400+ iterations per render, re-executed on every status cycle tap and every juz selection. The file's own comment (line 75) notes that exactly this kind of un-memoized 114-item filtering 'caused noticeable lag' and was fixed for the stats — but the juz chips were left unmemoized.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Precompute a static juz→surah-numbers map at module scope, and derive the per-juz memorized flags in a useMemo keyed on `progress`.

confirmed.title: 10 of 12 AdMob placements serve Google TEST ads in production (no revenue)
confirmed.description: resolveBanner() falls back to Google's public test banner unit whenever the EXPO_PUBLIC_ADMOB_BANNER_* env var is unset. The EAS 'production' environment (verified live via `npx eas-cli env:list --environment production`) contains ONLY EXPO_PUBLIC_ADMOB_BANNER_HOME, EXPO_PUBLIC_ADMOB_BANNER_STATS and EXPO_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN — the local .env has all 12 banner IDs but it is gitignored, so EAS builds never see it. Result: in the shipped production app, the Prayers tab, Tasbih, Learn, all six Guides screens, Sacred Journey, Dua, Names, Hifz, Mood and Qibla banners (AD_UNITS.bannerMood…bannerHifz) all resolve to the TEST_BANNER unit and earn zero revenue. Only Home and Stats run real ads. The .env.example only documents HOME/STATS, confirming the EAS env was set up before the other 10 placements were added and never updated.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add the 10 missing EXPO_PUBLIC_ADMOB_BANNER_* variables to the EAS production environment (eas env:create --environment production …), update .env.example to list all 12, and rebuild. Optionally add a startup dev-assert that logs/captures to Sentry when a production build resolves a TEST_BANNER unit.

confirmed.title: Quran fetches have no timeout and a hung request is trapped forever by the inflight dedup map
confirmed.description: All four Quran network calls (surah editions line 58, page reader line 200, IndoPak line 332, tajweed line 372) use bare fetch() with no AbortController/timeout and no retry. React Native fetch can hang indefinitely on flaky mobile networks (connection established, response never completes). Worse, fetchSurah/fetchPage memoize the in-flight promise in `inflight`/`pageInflight` maps and the entry is only deleted in `finally` — which never runs if the fetch never settles. So one hung request poisons that surah/page for the entire app session: every retry by the user awaits the same dead promise, and the reader shows the offline/loading state forever until app restart. This is the most direct code-level explanation for the top user complaint 'sometimes translation doesn't load'. Notably, src/utils/hijri.ts:97-101 already implements the correct AbortController+4s-timeout pattern, so the fix is known in this codebase.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Wrap every fetch in AbortController with a 10–15s timeout (as hijri.ts already does), add one retry with backoff, and ensure inflight entries are evicted on timeout so a user-initiated retry issues a fresh request.
confirmed.correction: Two minor nuances, neither refuting the claim: (1) "trapped forever until app restart" is fully true on Android (RN OkHttp has no read timeout), but on iOS NSURLSession's default ~60s request timeout would eventually reject the promise and clear the inflight entry, bounding the poisoning to about a minute per attempt. (2) On a hang, fetchSurah's caller shows the perpetual loading spinner, not the offline/error state — the catch branch never executes because the promise never settles.

confirmed.title: AdBanner permanently hides after a single ad-load failure for the whole session (keep-alive tabs)
confirmed.description: onAdFailedToLoad sets `failed=true` and the component then returns null forever — there is no retry, no backoff, and no reset on focus. Because the app's tab screens stay mounted (keep-alive architecture), one transient no-fill or network blip at cold start removes the banner from that screen for the entire session, silently cutting impressions. Combined with the test-unit fallback issue this compounds revenue loss.
confirmed.suggestedFix: On failure, schedule a retry with exponential backoff (e.g. 30s/60s/120s, capped attempts) by bumping a `key` on BannerAd, or reset `failed` on screen focus via useFocusEffect.

confirmed.title: GDPR/UMP consent flow: ads SDK init races the consent form, ad requests are never gated on canRequestAds, and there is no privacy-options re-entry point
confirmed.description: Three related gaps: (1) App.tsx wraps requestAdsConsent() in a 4-second race and then calls initAds() — an EEA user cannot read and answer the UMP form in 4s, so the Mobile Ads SDK initializes and AdBanner components mount and fire ad requests while consent is still undetermined (Google's UMP guidance: check canRequestAds() before requesting ads). (2) Nothing in the codebase ever checks consent state before rendering BannerAd — grep for canRequestAds returns zero hits outside consent.ts internals. (3) There is no AdsConsent.showPrivacyOptionsForm() entry point anywhere (grep confirms), so EEA users have no way to change/withdraw consent after the first launch, which Google's EU consent policy requires. Additionally any consent failure is silently swallowed (`catch {}`), and the `requested` flag is set before the attempt, so a failed requestInfoUpdate is never retried within the session.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Await consent (no artificial 4s cap on the form itself — only on requestInfoUpdate), gate initAds()/AdBanner rendering on AdsConsent.getConsentInfo().canRequestAds, and add a 'Privacy options' row in the Sidebar that calls AdsConsent.showPrivacyOptionsForm() when privacyOptionsRequirementStatus is REQUIRED.
confirmed.correction: Two minor refinements that strengthen rather than weaken the claim: (1) canRequestAds has zero hits everywhere, including inside consent.ts itself — the claim's "outside consent.ts internals" wording understated this. (2) The consent.ts:39 comment asserting ads "fall back to non-personalised" on consent failure is itself inaccurate: no code anywhere sets a non-personalized-ads flag (AdBanner's requestOptions contains only keywords), so that fallback exists only as a comment. The only mitigation found is AdBanner hiding on onAdFailedToLoad, which is UI degradation, not consent gating — the ungated request is still sent.

confirmed.title: Sentry only ever sees React render crashes — every operational failure is swallowed by silent catch blocks
confirmed.description: captureError() is invoked from exactly one place in the entire app: the ErrorBoundary in App.tsx (verified by grep). All the failure paths users actually complain about — Quran fetch failures (quran.ts has 10+ bare `catch {}` / `catch { return null }` blocks), consent failures, ads init failures, review-prompt failures, notification scheduling failures (App.tsx lines 446/462 `} catch {}`) — are silently discarded and never reach Sentry. The developer is flying blind on exactly the class of intermittent failures users report ('sometimes random things fail'), because the DSN is configured and working but nothing is reported except hard render crashes.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Route the meaningful silent catches (Quran network failure after retries, notification-scheduling failure, ads/consent init failure) through captureError with a tag, or at minimum Sentry.addBreadcrumb, so production failure modes become visible. Keep truly-expected offline cases as breadcrumbs rather than events to control quota.
confirmed.correction: Two corrections: (1) The title "Sentry only ever sees React render crashes" is inaccurate — Sentry.init (App.tsx:65) with default integrations plus Sentry.wrap(AppInner) (App.tsx:534) also captures unhandled JS exceptions, unhandled promise rejections, and native crashes. The accurate claim is narrower: errors swallowed by the app's many silent catch blocks (quran.ts x15, App.tsx notification/location blocks, consent.ts, ads.ts, review.ts) never reach Sentry because they never become unhandled, and captureError is never invoked outside the ErrorBoundary. (2) Several of these silences are documented, deliberate offline-tolerance fallbacks (e.g. quran.ts lines 341-343, 381-383 explain falling back instead of crashing; the Sentry.init comment shows intentionally narrow Sentry scope for the privacy claim), so part of the gap is a design trade-off rather than pure oversight — reporting offline Quran fetches would mostly be noise. The real observability gap is in paths like consent, ads init, review prompt, and notification scheduling, where intermittent failures are invisible. Medium severity is roughly fair for those.

confirmed.title: Sentry Expo plugin and metro config missing — no source maps, production stack traces are minified
confirmed.description: @sentry/react-native ~7.2.0 is installed and Sentry.init runs, but app.json's plugins array contains no "@sentry/react-native/expo" entry and there is no metro.config.js in the repo (verified: `ls metro.config.js` → not found), so getSentryExpoConfig is never applied. Without the config plugin + metro wrapper, debug IDs are not injected and source maps are never uploaded — every JS error that does reach Sentry has a minified, effectively useless stack trace (`index.android.bundle:1:483921`). The presence of SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN in .env.local shows source-map upload was intended but never wired up.
confirmed.evidence:
app.json plugins: ["expo-font", ["expo-notifications",...], ["expo-location",...], "expo-asset", ["react-native-google-mobile-ads",...], "./plugins/withAdiRegistration", "./plugins/withStripActivityRecognition"]  — no @sentry/react-native/expo

$ ls metro.config.js → "no metro.config.js"

.env.local: SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN=sntrys_...
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add ["@sentry/react-native/expo", { organization, project }] to app.json plugins, create metro.config.js using getSentryExpoConfig from @sentry/react-native/metro, and provide SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN as an EAS secret so release builds upload source maps.

confirmed.title: Over-declared Android permissions: USE_EXACT_ALARM (Play-restricted) and FOREGROUND_SERVICE (unused)
confirmed.description: app.json declares both SCHEDULE_EXACT_ALARM and USE_EXACT_ALARM plus FOREGROUND_SERVICE. Google Play policy restricts USE_EXACT_ALARM to apps whose core function is an alarm clock or calendar; other apps must use SCHEDULE_EXACT_ALARM (user-revocable) and are flagged/rejected at review for declaring USE_EXACT_ALARM — a concrete update-rejection risk for the next Play submission. FOREGROUND_SERVICE is declared but the codebase contains no foreground service at all (grep for ForegroundService/foregroundService across src/ and App.tsx returns nothing), and since Android 14 any FGS also requires a typed permission, so this declaration is pure review-friction with zero benefit.
confirmed.evidence:
"permissions": [
  ...
  "android.permission.WAKE_LOCK",
  "android.permission.FOREGROUND_SERVICE",
  "android.permission.SCHEDULE_EXACT_ALARM",
  "android.permission.USE_EXACT_ALARM",
  ...
]

$ grep -rn "ForegroundService\|foregroundService" src App.tsx app.json → no hits
confirmed.suggestedFix: Remove USE_EXACT_ALARM and FOREGROUND_SERVICE from app.json android.permissions; keep SCHEDULE_EXACT_ALARM (with a runtime canScheduleExactAlarms fallback to inexact alarms) and RECEIVE_BOOT_COMPLETED for expo-notifications restore.
confirmed.correction: Two characterization fixes: (1) kind should be policy/compliance, not reliability — nothing malfunctions at runtime; this is purely a Play review/policy exposure. (2) "Concrete update-rejection risk" is somewhat overstated: the app is already at versionCode 12, so earlier builds declaring USE_EXACT_ALARM have evidently passed Play review, and a prayer-times app scheduling adhan alarms has an arguable (though not enumerated) case under the alarm-functionality exception. It is a genuine flagging risk on future submissions and the permission is unnecessary alongside SCHEDULE_EXACT_ALARM, but rejection is not certain. The FOREGROUND_SERVICE portion of the claim is fully accurate: declared, completely unused, safe to remove.

confirmed.title: iOS AdMob app ID in app.json is Google's public sample ID
confirmed.description: The react-native-google-mobile-ads plugin config uses the real publisher app ID for Android (ca-app-pub-6562347670701476~...) but Google's documented sample app ID (ca-app-pub-3940256099942544~1458002511) for iOS. The app is currently Android-only on Play, but the moment an iOS build ships, all ads run against Google's demo app and earn nothing — and it's the kind of value that looks configured at a glance.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Replace iosAppId with the real AdMob iOS app ID before any iOS release, or add a comment/TODO marker so it is not shipped accidentally.
confirmed.correction: Minor nuance only: with a sample app ID, the Google Mobile Ads SDK serves test ads (or fails to fill against real ad units) rather than routing revenue to "Google's demo app" — the practical outcome is the same (zero revenue on iOS), but ads would not literally run and earn for a demo property.

confirmed.title: expo-av is deprecated in SDK 54 (removed in SDK 55) — Quran audio player sits on a dead-end API
confirmed.description: The Quran audio playback (src/hooks/useAudioPlayer.ts, src/components/AyahSheet.tsx, streaming from everyayah.com via Audio.Sound.createAsync) depends on expo-av ~16.0.8. Expo deprecated expo-av in SDK 54 and removes it in SDK 55, so the next Expo SDK upgrade (often forced by Play target-API requirements) breaks ayah audio unless migrated to expo-audio.
confirmed.evidence:
package.json: "expo": "~54.0.34", "expo-av": "~16.0.8"
useAudioPlayer.ts:284: const { sound } = await Audio.Sound.createAsync({ uri: url }, { shouldPlay: false });
confirmed.suggestedFix: Plan a migration of useAudioPlayer/AyahSheet to expo-audio before upgrading to SDK 55; the preload/unload lifecycle maps cleanly onto expo-audio's createAudioPlayer API.
confirmed.correction: Minor timeline detail: expo-av was deprecated in SDK 53, not SDK 54 (SDK 54 is merely the last SDK that ships it). Also, SDK 55 is no longer "the next upgrade" in the abstract — it has already been released with expo-av fully removed, so the risk is more concrete than the claim implies. Everything else is accurate.

confirmed.title: Unused dependency @react-navigation/drawer shipped in the bundle
confirmed.description: @react-navigation/drawer ^7.9.4 is declared as a dependency but is imported nowhere in the app (grep across src/, App.tsx, index.ts finds zero imports — the app uses a custom Sidebar component instead). It adds dead JS weight and an extra package to keep in sync across SDK upgrades.
confirmed.evidence:
package.json: "@react-navigation/drawer": "^7.9.4"
$ grep -rln "react-navigation/drawer" src App.tsx index.ts → no hits
confirmed.suggestedFix: npm uninstall @react-navigation/drawer.
confirmed.correction: The dependency is unused, but the "dead JS weight in the bundle" framing is inaccurate: Metro bundles only modules reachable from the entry point, so an unimported package adds zero bytes to the shipped JS bundle, and @react-navigation/drawer is pure JS with no native module, so no autolinking/APK impact either. The actual cost is node_modules size, install time, and maintenance/upgrade surface — a dependency-hygiene issue (kind: maintenance) rather than perf. Severity low is correct.

confirmed.title: Sentry performance tracing (tracesSampleRate 0.2) contradicts the in-app privacy claim of 'crash reports only'
confirmed.description: The Sentry.init comment explicitly says the listing claims 'no tracking, data stays on device' with anonymous crash reports as the only exception, and disables logs for that reason — yet tracesSampleRate: 0.2 enables performance transaction reporting (app-start timings, device/OS metadata on 20% of sessions), which is not a crash report. The in-app About text (src/components/Sidebar.tsx:322) likewise tells users only 'anonymous crash reports' leave the device. This is an inconsistency between stated privacy posture and actual telemetry, relevant to the Play Data Safety form.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Set tracesSampleRate: 0 (crash-only posture, matching the privacy text), or update the in-app disclosure and Play Data Safety form to mention anonymous performance diagnostics.
